suppliers.note: E&T client. FreeStyle Libre 2 Plus / 3 Plus sensors, LibreView. On the Insulin Pumps, CGM & HCL framework (2025/S 000-4043) to 31/05/2027, ~£1bn/4yr.

voice.line: Lead with patient outcomes and the cost of poor glucose control — clinical evidence first, then whole-system savings (fewer admissions, less staff time). Not a price play.

suppliers._specialitiesEvidence: Framework-verified: 'Insulin Pumps, Continuous Glucose Monitoring, Products Contributing to the Delivery of Hybrid Closed Loop Pathways and Associated Products' (2025/S 000-4043, Medical and Surgical Consumables) — Abbott Laboratories Ltd named among 12 awarded suppliers. NOT tagged Pathology and laboratory: Abbott Laboratories Ltd / Abbott Rapid Diagnostics Ltd / Abbott Toxicology Ltd are awarded on the Lab Diagnostics/POCT framework, but those read as separate Abbott business divisions from this specific 'Abbott Diabetes Care' supplier record, whose catalogued products are diabetes-only. FreeStyle Optium Neo confirmed via NHS dm+d as a Drug Tariff item — a different route (community prescribing), not NHSSC — noted, not tagged as a separate speciality.

alerts.title: Class 2 Medicines Recall: ChloraPrep 1mL applicators, EL(26)A/34 (13/07/2026) — the fourth ChloraPrep recall since May 2025
alerts.detail: Recalled 13/07/2026 for a potential breach of sterility in the packaging process (all lots with expiry up to 05/2029); contamination "could lead to increased infection rates". Follows EL(26)A/26 (02/06/2026, 1mL and Frepp 1.5mL, same sterility reason) and two 2025 recalls for open applicator seals (EL(25)A/22, EL(25)A/36).
alerts.use: If ChloraPrep is the incumbent, continuity of licensed supply is the conversation — a licensed second source de-risks the category.

alerts.title: Venflon IV cannula with wings, 14G–22G
alerts.detail: Discontinued through rationalisation; progressive delisting since Jan 2025, all codes delisted by 31/07/2026.
alerts.use: Any trust still on Venflon must convert now — an immediate switching conversation for every cannula supplier.

alerts.title: 4Fr single-lumen PowerPICC (SOLO and non-SOLO)
alerts.detail: Global product removal for material-fatigue leak risk (FSN updated 15/05/2025); unused stock destroyed.
alerts.use: Context for PICC conversations — ask what the trust moved to and whether the replacement is bedding in well.

suppliers.note: Full portfolio from BD’s UK sites (bd.com/en-uk, bardcare.uk). NHS Supply Chain framework status per product still to confirm — many lines are sold DIRECT / off-framework (Biosciences flow cytometry, Rowa, EnCor, Pyxis/Alaris capital). Diabetes needles excluded (Embecta spin-off 2022).

voice.line: Sell on clinical evidence, patient and staff safety, and standardisation across the system; efficiency and total cost of ownership over unit price.

suppliers._specialitiesEvidence: Multi-speciality review 20/07/2026: Vacutainer / Eclipse / Safety-Lok specimen collection, BACTEC blood culture.

frameworks.name: NHS Supply Chain — Syringes & Needles
frameworks.note: 47 suppliers (25 new)

voice.line: Broad brand-led everyday clinical consumables; sells on breadth and trusted brands across ward/theatre.

suppliers._specialitiesEvidence: Framework-verified — all four read from live NHS Supply Chain contract launch briefs, Cardinal Health UK 432 named as an awarded supplier on each: 'Enteral Feeding, Bile Bags and Associated Products' (covers Kangaroo pumps/sets) -> Nutrition and enteral feeding. 'Suction, Wound Drainage, Autologous Blood Systems and Related Consumables' (covers Argyle drainage) -> Theatres / surgical (best available fit; no dedicated 'surgical drainage' speciality exists). 'Electrodes, Ultrasound Gels, Defibrillation and Related Consumables' -> Patient monitoring (verified award; not reflected in the currently catalogued product list, included as genuine market presence). 'Syringes, Needles and Associated Products' (covers Monoject) -> Vascular access, following the same convention used for GBUK's own 'Needles & Syringes' filing. NOT framework-verified but kept as product-only inferences: Wound care (Kerlix/Curity/Telfa — checked against the General Wound Care framework, Cardinal Health NOT listed there) and Continence / Urology (Dover Foley catheters — checked against Urology and Bowel Management, NOT listed there). Both are well-known branded dressing/catheter lines; the framework search coming back empty may be an enumeration gap rather than genuine absence from the market, but treat these two as the weaker half of this supplier's tags. ALSO FOUND, not a tag: explicitly DELISTED from the 'Surgical Instruments' framework (confirmed real and current, not historical) — recorded for accuracy, since a rep should not expect them there.

frameworks.name: NHS TPN framework
frameworks.note: named supplier (FTS 040414-2025)

suppliers.note: On extension sets and IV accessories.

voice.line: "Caring for life" — non-PVC/DEHP-free safety + coordinated IV therapy and nutrition.

suppliers._specialitiesEvidence: Framework-verified: 'Infusion Pumps and Administration Sets and Associated Products' (Project_12 ITT_382, Medical and Surgical Consumables) names 'Fresenius Kabi' directly — covers Agilia Connect/Volumat/Injectomat and the Dedicated Volumat lines -> Vascular access. Also awarded on 'Enteral Feeding, Bile Bags and Associated Products' (2025/S 000-028317) -> Nutrition and enteral feeding, included as genuine market presence though not reflected in the currently catalogued product list. Corroborated by this record's own pre-existing 'frameworks' entry ('NHS TPN framework', FTS 040414-2025) — that entry's framework NAME (TPN) and its own descriptive note ('extension sets and IV accessories') don't quite match each other, which is itself a useful flag: whatever FTS 040414-2025 actually covers, it sits closer to the Infusion Pumps/Administration Sets finding above than to true parenteral nutrition. SmofKabiven / Kabiven / SMOFlipid (parenteral nutrition) are confirmed via NHS LPP / NOE CPC as a regional PHARMACY MEDICINES procurement route (framework ref LPP/2022/001) — NOT an NHS Supply Chain Category Business Unit. Covered loosely by the 'Nutrition and enteral feeding' tag above (the label's 'Nutrition' half is broad enough to reasonably include PN), though the tag is genuinely closer-fitting for tube feeding than IV nutrition — flagged rather than hidden. freeflex/freeflex+ IV bags are likely on a similar national medicines framework (CM/PHR prefix) but this could not be confirmed — Find a Tender blocked the fetch.

suppliers._specialitiesEvidence: Framework-verified from two independent procurement bodies, both naming this exact product line (ULT freezers, blood bank refrigerators, plasma freezers, vaccine/pharmacy fridges, biological safety cabinets, CO2 incubators): (1) NHS Supply Chain 'Laboratory Diagnostics, Point of Care Testing and Pathology Managed Services' (2023/S 000-028831, Diagnostic Equipment and Services) — Haier Biomedical named among 122 awarded suppliers. (2) NHS National Services Scotland 'Medical Refrigeration and Cold Chain Equipment' (NP176/25) and 'Vaccine Refrigeration and Transportation' (NP176/21) — a different procurement body from NHSSC (England/Wales), but confirming the same speciality via the exact product families on file. Not England-only, worth knowing when planning a UK-wide approach to this supplier. | Multi-speciality review 20/07/2026: Blood bank refrigerators -- storage for the transfusion/blood-bank function.

suppliers.name: Merit Medical UK Ltd

frameworks.name: NHS Supply Chain — Syringes & Needles
frameworks.note: 47 suppliers (25 new)

voice.line: Procedure-specialist, evidence-led for interventional radiology/cardiology.

suppliers._specialitiesEvidence: Framework-verified via the downloaded NHS Supply Chain Product Matrix for 'Interventional Cardiology, Interventional Radiology and Interventional Neuroradiology, Cardiac Rhythm Management and Electrophysiology' (2021/S 000-017565, Medical Technology): Merit Medical UK Limited = Yes on Lot 1 (Interventional Cardiology/Radiology, covering Prelude/PreludeSYNC/Embosphere) and Lot 2 (Interventional Neuroradiology, covering SwiftNINJA) -> Cardiology + Neurosciences. Vascular access added from this record's own PRE-EXISTING 'frameworks' entry ('NHS Supply Chain — Syringes & Needles', £250m/3yr, from 01/07/2026, 47 suppliers) — identical wording to Cardinal Health's entry for the same framework, which reads as a templated note from a prior bulk scrape rather than hand-written prose, but the underlying award is independently plausible (same framework, same figures) and follows the same Syringes/Needles -> Vascular access convention used elsewhere. Not found independently by this round of research. DELIBERATELY NOT tagged from the CVC framework, and this is a real catch confirmed by BOTH sources: the research AND this record's own pre-existing note ('Delisted from the CVC framework') agree Merit Medical is explicitly DELISTED from 'Central Venous Catheters and Associated Products' at its 23/03/2026 start. Their actual products (arterial access sheaths, post-catheterisation compression) are cath-lab/interventional tools, a different category from the peripheral/central venous access our 'Vascular access' speciality's CVC framework covers — worth knowing the tag above comes from a different, genuinely-relevant framework (Syringes & Needles), not the CVC one. NOT TAGGED, flagged as genuine gaps: HeRO Graft (a haemodialysis access graft — 'Renal and dialysis' is the obvious clinical fit by product identity, but the Renal Replacement Therapies framework was checked and does not list Merit Medical) and SCOUT breast localisation (no NHSSC framework found under any search angle). Both left untagged rather than forced, per the same standard applied throughout this exercise.

suppliers.note: Genuine dual player, unusually for this speciality: Preanalytics business unit carries BOTH VACUETTE blood collection AND the CLiP peripheral IV cannula range (14G-26G, ported/winged) plus a SWiTCH arterial catheter — one of only two suppliers in the system tagged both Vascular access and Blood collection. Two Greiner Bio-One business units overall (Preanalytics, BioScience); BioScience is research/industry-facing, not relevant to NHS ward sales. Verified 20/07/2026 against greiner.com and the live gbo.com catalogue.

voice.line: The same Preanalytics contact covers both blood collection and peripheral IV access — a rep can consolidate two conversations most competitors keep separate.

frameworks.name: NHS Supply Chain — Blood Collection Devices (2024/S 000-033791)
frameworks.note: Awarded supplier

frameworks.name: NHS Supply Chain — Blood Draw Tools and Accessories (2024/S 000-009366)
frameworks.note: Awarded — lancets lot

suppliers.note: Specimen-collection and laboratory company, NOT a vascular access player — no IV cannula found anywhere in the current published catalogue (verified against the live site 20/07/2026, with the caveat that the site is mid-migration and some legacy pages are unreachable, so 'none found' rather than a flat denial). Four business areas: Preanalytics, Life Science, Laboratory automation, Sample transport. S-Monovette's differentiator is that the same device supports EITHER aspiration (gentler, adjusts to vein condition) OR a fresh user-generated vacuum — not a factory pre-set vacuum that decays.

voice.line: Aspiration technique reduces haemolysis and repeat draws — lead with patient experience and sample quality, not price.
